description_long: This cowshed is a large, rectangular agricultural building with a steeply pitched gable roof, clad in weathered wooden shingles. The roof features prominent crossed wooden finials at both ends, a traditional detail often seen in Scandinavian or Northern European rural architecture. The structure is supported by a series of evenly spaced vertical posts along the perimeter, creating an open-sided design that allows for ventilation and easy access for livestock. The exterior is predominantly grayish-brown, with the wood showing signs of age and exposure to the elements. The base is surrounded by a patch of grass, suggesting its placement in a rural or semi-urban environment. No windows are visible, and the main entry appears to be a wide, open section beneath the roof overhang.
description: A large, open-sided wooden cowshed with a steep gable roof and traditional crossed finials, supported by vertical posts and surrounded by grass. The weathered wood exterior gives it a rustic, rural appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the steep gable roof with crossed finials and the open entry supported by vertical posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, continuous roofline and the open-sided structure with multiple supporting posts.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the gable roof and crossed finials visible, but no clear entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the extended roof and open sides supported by posts.
attribute_door_style: wide open entry, no visible door, framed by wooden posts
